title: potentilla × subnivalis
intro: Potentilla × subnivalis, also known as the Tibetan cinquefoil, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the rose family and is native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been found to possess medicinal value in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Potentilla × subnivalis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatism, and act as an antimicrobial agent, making it a potential remedy for skin conditions and infections. Potentilla × subnivalis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
